title: Mybench Descriptor Registry
description: The versioned, published descriptor registry (MYB-10.2, handoff §7): the single source of truth for what mybench can claim (entries: band edges, min-support, inference risk, disclosure flags, presets) and what it deliberately does NOT claim (rejected: section, banned framings). Strict whitelist like every mybench artifact. Cross-entry rules the schema cannot express live in mybench.registry: unique ids, employer-safe preset entries must be R0, internal-feature-only entries carry no presets and are structurally non-renderable, reserved entries are not claimable.

vocabulary_id.description: Stable identifier for a pinned taxonomy or vocabulary; semantics change only with its separately declared version.

cell_id.description: Closed identifier from a pinned taxonomy; never free text.

support_thresholds.description: Minimum evidence volume for one conditioning cell. Every threshold is positive: a cell below any declared floor is absent, never emitted as zero.

registry_version.description: Version of the registry document as a whole; any entry change bumps it. The file's git history is the audit trail for 'what did this descriptor mean at time T'.

format_status.description: Serialization status. JSON was ratified by ADR-0016 at the MYB-10.17 sitting; the other values remain readable only for historical registry artifacts.

id.description: namespace.name; the registry_id claims cite. Never a cognitive-trait framing (banned_framings, checked by the loader).

version.description: Semver of this entry; band recalibration = version bump + retroactive re-disclosure, no code change (handoff §7 rule 1-2).

class.description: Derivation class the entry's claims must carry (handoff §5).

status.description: active = scorers may emit claims against it now; reserved = id + shape reserved (judge family, later waves, roadmap §6 section placeholders) — the loader rejects claims against reserved entries.

wave.description: Delivery wave from the handoff §6 catalog (1-3); 0 = roadmap-§6 fingerprint-section placeholder owned by MYB-13.x.

inputs.description: Named input classes (normalized evidence streams), not paths.

output_schema.description: JSON-schema fragment the claim's output object must validate against — exact shape lives HERE, not in scorer code.

band_definitions.description: Coarse band edges (handoff §8.1: bands, not point values). May be empty for an active exact aggregate whose closed output schema contains no enum band surface.

min_support.description: Minimum evidence volume per unit; below threshold NO claim is emitted, never a zero-valued claim (handoff §8.4, THREAT_MODEL §3.3). per_conditioning_cell keys are the closed cells admitted for a conditioned entry and each value is that cell's independent support floor. Empty only while reserved.

conditioning_axis.description: Optional conditioning axis for segmented claims. The taxonomy identity and version pin cell semantics. Active conditioned entries must require an enumerated string output.condition and declare a matching independent min_support.per_conditioning_cell floor for every admitted cell (loader-enforced).

conditional_denominator.description: Optional declaration for metrics whose denominator is a conditioned population (for example, errors the pinned rubric classified as catchable). Names the closed condition class and the version-bound source that supplies the denominator; it never embeds observations.

severity_weight_vocabulary.description: Optional pinned severity vocabulary for conditionally weighted judge metrics. The registry declares only class identifiers; numeric weights are deliberately not part of this closed shape and remain versioned judge-rubric behavior (MYB-7.1).

inference_risk.description: R0 safe aggregate; R1 contextual leak in combination; R2 potentially identifying in combination (handoff §8.3).

disclosure.description: public entries may enter a publication preview after its separate gates; local-report-only entries may render only in the private A10 report; internal-feature-only descriptors are STRUCTURALLY NON-RENDERABLE everywhere (handoff §7.1, ADR-0019).

report_location.description: Closed report-v2 destination for an atomic claim. Absence means the entry is not admitted to report v2; renderers never infer a location from an id or note.

report_controls.description: Registry-owned activation state for report-v2 optional metadata. Every report-located entry declares all three switches; false keeps the schema-reserved shape semantically unreachable.

caveat_copy.description: Plain-language renderer copy keyed by every controlled caveat code required by output_schema. Report-located entries declare an exact key match; wrappers carry codes only.

presets.description: Opt-in bundles that include this entry. employer-safe = R0 only (loader-enforced). internal-feature-only entries must have no presets.

neutrality_note.description: Per-entry honesty rule: characterizes, never grades; banned deficit framings recorded here (handoff §8.2/§8.6).

risk_note.description: Plain-language risk note, REQUIRED for R1/R2 entries (loader-enforced): what the disclosure can leak in combination (handoff §8.3).

rejected.description: Documented non-claims (handoff §7.2) — a transparency artifact: disclosures deliberately not made, each with its rationale.

banned_framings.description: Strings banned as descriptor titles, ids, or copy anywhere in the product (handoff §7.2/§8.6); loader-enforced against entry ids and titles.
